core_purpose: Flight Manual is an open-source, code-driven API documentation engine built on Astro Starlight and Cloudflare Pages, positioned as a free alternative to Mintlify. It deterministically generates MDX reference docs from TypeScript Zod schemas, ships an interactive Scalar API playground, an llms.txt build artifact, and an auto-generated MCP server.

main_features.name: Zod Schema → MDX API Reference Codegen
Stateful AI Chat Agent (Durable Object + Workers AI)

technical_debt: No automated test suite exists (only a linkinator link-checker script). The /api/chat handler returns a hardcoded simulated stream and the production Durable Object path is commented-out scaffolding (functions/api/chat.ts:14-21). functions/agent.ts contains an explicit TODO for stream consumption/state persistence and falls back to mocked responses when the AI binding is missing. Enterprise RBAC middleware accepts a literal '?token=secret' query string and a static 'FM_ENTERPRISE_TOKEN=authorized' cookie — placeholder auth, not production. Repository state is messy: a stale astro.config.mjs.bak, a committed _worker.bundle artifact at repo root, package-lock.json deleted while pnpm-lock.yaml is added (uncommitted, mixed package-manager signal), and broken-link-checker is declared in devDependencies but never invoked. README still contains a literal '[TODO]' placeholder for the demo GIF, and wrangler.jsonc is absent despite Cloudflare Workers/DO usage (no bindings config in repo).
active_roadmap: No open GitHub issues — no externally tracked roadmap. Implicit next steps inferred from in-code TODOs: wire the DocsAgent Durable Object binding and finish the streaming/storage path in functions/agent.ts; replace the demo token-based RBAC in functions/_middleware.ts with real auth; add a wrangler.jsonc with Vectorize, AI, and DO bindings; introduce a test suite.
